behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s content selection quality (LLM-DEP=yes): a stubbed model can always echo fixtures and hide generic real-model outputs. No real OS/network/subprocess is required beyond the model call itself, and this behavior is about one model decision (not a multi-agent chain), so Agentic is the minimum over System/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real draft-generation LLM on representative email threads and assert the reply cites thread-specific details (entities, commitments, or questions) rather than generic boilerplate across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text does not require model-quality judgment; it can be tested as module handoff (draft-claim extraction/checker + KB lookup) with in-process fixtures (MOCK-MISS=yes, REAL-INFRA=no). Rejected higher levels: no explicit text saying the check depends on real AI/ML output quality (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow), and no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess (disqualifies System).
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft parser/checker with a seeded local KB fixture and verify contradictory claims are flagged and surfaced to the next stage; include negative controls where matching claims are not flagged.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality/behavior-steering of generation under missing-context conditions (LLM-DEP=yes). A deterministic stub would always include the disclaimer and hide hallucination risk (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Not Workflow because the core pass/fail is a single generation decision given empty retrieval context.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real generation with empty/irrelevant KB retrieval results and assert the produced draft contains the required uncertainty/disclaimer language and avoids unsupported factual assertions.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic orchestration/state-gating across modules (approval state + send service). In-process doubles for sender and UI event bus can catch real handoff bugs (MOCK-MISS=yes, REAL-INFRA=no). Rejected System: behavior text does not require real SMTP/API transport semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output-quality dependence in the gate condition.
behaviors.test_description: Run the approval state machine with a mocked send adapter: verify send is never invoked before explicit approval event, then invoked exactly once after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly validates a multi-step pipeline with at least one real LLM-dependent stage and sequential handoffs (MULTI-STEP=yes, LLM-DEP=yes). Lower levels miss cross-step failures (context loss, incorrect stage transitions, approval gating after checks).
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a real email thread through all stages (ingest, generate via real LLM, fact-check, approval presentation) and assert correct artifacts and transitions at each boundary, with no send before approval.
